MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: Data Center compounding at 66-68% YoY through FY26; Q1 FY27 guided $43B with 73.5% GM despite $5.5B H20 China write-off.

shifts.trend: Q3 FY26 Data Center $51.2B (+66% YoY); FY26 full-year Data Center +68% YoY — Blackwell majority of DC revenue by H2 FY26
shifts.note: Blackwell platform ramp driving acceleration; Networking +162% QoQ as NVLink cluster demand grows

shifts.trend: China revenue: Q3 FY25 $8.1B → Q3 FY26 $3.0B; $4.5B H20 inventory charge Q1 FY26; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% tariff
shifts.note: H20 sales near-zero post April 2025 controls; China now <6% of revenue vs ~20% prior

shifts.trend: Q4 FY26 guided $37.5B; Q1 FY27 guided $43.0B ± 2% with 73.5% GM — sequential step up from Q4 beat
shifts.note: Q1 FY27 guidance implies +15%+ sequential; Rubin platform in 2026 sustains annual cadence

shifts.trend: $7.5B new data center lease obligations (FY26 10-K) + $3.5B land/power guarantees; $17.5B invested in private AI infra cos
shifts.note: NVDA is now funding AI infrastructure alongside GPU sales — balance sheet deployed for supply lock-in

NVDA.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening: Blackwell ramp removes supply concerns, Rubin maintains moat cadence. China effectively zeroed without damage to growth trajectory. $43B Q1 FY27 guide at 73.5% GM confirms pricing power at scale.

CSCO.headline: Networking accelerating from +15% to +21% QoQ on Silicon One AI infrastructure demand; FY26 raised to $60.2-61.2B.

shifts.trend: Networking product category: Q1 FY26 +15% → Q2 FY26 +21% YoY; total revenue Q1 +8% → Q2 +10%
shifts.note: AI Infrastructure and Data Center Switching leading; Service Provider and Cloud hyperscaler orders strengthening

shifts.trend: FY26 revenue guidance raised to $60.2-61.2B (from $59-60B at Q1); Q3 FY26 guided $15.4-15.6B
shifts.note: Raised guidance reflects Silicon One hyperscaler momentum; Security softness (-4%) a partial offset

shifts.trend: Silicon One contract manufacturing commitments building both quarters; purchase commitments +10% at Q1; still growing at Q2
shifts.note: Hyperscaler concentration in Cisco networking spend — supply-chain exposure to single product family

CSCO.verdict: Thesis intact: AI networking recovery is real and accelerating. Security is a drag but networking is 30%+ of revenue and growing at 21% YoY. FY26 raise signals management conviction. Long-term Cisco AI infrastructure thesis building.

AVGO.headline: AI XPU semi solutions +52% YoY in Q1 FY26; top-5 customer concentration jumped to 50% vs 40% a year ago — single hyperscaler dominance.

shifts.trend: AI revenue: FY25 $12.2B; Semi solutions: FY25 +22% → Q1 FY26 +52% YoY
shifts.note: Custom XPU and AI networking (Tomahawk/Jericho) both accelerating into FY26

shifts.trend: Top-5 customer share: FY25 ~40% → Q1 FY26 ~50%; one distributor 29% → 42% of Q1 FY26 revenue
shifts.note: Single hyperscaler XPU pull-through dominates prints; positive for near-term but key risk if relationship shifts

shifts.trend: Gross margin: FY25 68% → Q1 FY26 68%; operating margin 40% FY25 → 44% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: XPU rack-sale/leasing model flagged as future gross margin headwind; software VMware mix stabilizing blended GM

shifts.trend: Semi solutions: FY25 58% of revenue → Q1 FY26 65% of revenue; Infrastructure software declining as % of mix
shifts.note: AI-driven semi mix shift reduces software contribution percentage even as software revenue stays flat

AVGO.verdict: Thesis intact: AVGO is among the biggest AI semi winners via custom XPU. Concentration risk is elevated but reflects real demand. 50% top-5 customer dependency warrants monitoring — any XPU program pivot would materially impact revenue.

MRVL.headline: Data Center 74% of FY26 revenue (+46% YoY); Celestial AI and XConn acquisitions extend into silicon photonics and CXL — roadmap building toward CPO.

shifts.trend: Data Center end-market: Q3 FY26 +38% YoY → FY26 full year +46% YoY; total revenue Q3 +37% → FY26 +42%
shifts.note: Custom ASIC and electro-optics both growing; Communications recovering from inventory trough

shifts.trend: Distributor A (hyperscaler conduit): Q3 FY26 38% → FY26 full year 37%; top-10 ~81% of revenue historically
shifts.note: Concentration persisting at high levels; two acquisitions (Celestial, XConn) will add new customer segments

shifts.trend: Celestial AI ~$1.3B cash + 24.5M shares (Feb 2026); XConn $280M + 2.1M shares (Feb 2026) — both post-FY26 year-end
shifts.note: Combined ~$1.6B+ cash deployed for photonic fabric and CXL switching — building CPO capability stack

shifts.trend: Data Center 73% of Q3 FY26 → 74% of FY26 full year; Communications recovering from ~0% to +31% growth
shifts.note: Mix concentration continuing; Infineon automotive sale ($2.5B) completed Aug 2025 — automotive revenue zeroed

MRVL.verdict: Thesis intact and building: MRVL's custom ASIC + silicon photonics + CPO stack is the most complete inference moat play after NVDA. Celestial/XConn acquisitions accelerate CPO roadmap. Concentration risk manageable given hyperscaler demand durability.

ORCL.headline: OCI cloud infrastructure +68% → +84% QoQ acceleration; RPO $523B → $553B; 9-month capex $39.2B (3x YoY) — contracting velocity unprecedented.

shifts.trend: OCI cloud infrastructure: Q2 FY26 $4.08B (+68% YoY) → Q3 FY26 $4.89B (+84% YoY)
shifts.note: AI workload demand driving double-digit acceleration in OCI revenue; no signs of deceleration

shifts.trend: 9M FY26 capex $39.2B vs $12.1B 9M FY25 (3.2x); H1 FY26 alone $20.5B vs $6.3B H1 FY25
shifts.note: Upward trajectory to continue 'next few fiscal years'; funded by $42.7B senior notes + $5B mandatory convert + ATM

shifts.trend: RPO: Q2 FY26 $523.3B (5.4x Nov 2024) → Q3 FY26 $552.6B (4.2x Feb 2025) — contract signings compounding
shifts.note: RPO concentration from a small number of very large hyperscaler cloud contracts — single-customer risks

shifts.trend: Debt: $17.9B new notes Sept 2025 + $42.7B notes total + $5B mandatory convert = $48B+ new issuance FY26
shifts.note: Balance sheet fully deployed; margin pressure from infra spend ahead of revenue recognition

ORCL.verdict: Thesis intact but balance sheet risk elevated. OCI growth +84% YoY is exceptional and RPO growth confirms real demand. However, $48B+ in new debt with margin compression requires long-duration belief in the AI cloud buildout cycle.

HPE.headline: Networking +51% (FY25) → +151.5% (Q1 FY26) on Juniper full-quarter inclusion; AI server growth at 'limited margins' — GM expansion from software mix.

shifts.trend: Networking segment: FY25 +51.1% → Q1 FY26 +151.5% YoY (Juniper full vs partial quarter prior-year)
shifts.note: Run-rate Networking growth will normalize in Q2 FY26 as comparables include Juniper; organic Juniper growth the true metric

shifts.trend: Gross margin: FY25 ~29% → Q1 FY26 35.9% (+670bps YoY); non-GAAP op margin 9.8% (FY25) → 12.7% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Juniper software/services mix expanding blended GM; AI server 'limited margins' due to GPU pass-through pricing partially offset

shifts.trend: $600M synergy target (FY28) requires $800M investment; $350M Catalyst cost program targeting FY27 savings
shifts.note: Integration costs $123M in Q1 FY26 alone; memory supply constraints flagged as new risk in Q1

shifts.trend: ARR $3.15B (+63% FY25); Juniper aaS portfolio adding subscription revenue; Cloud & AI segment consolidated in Q1 FY26
shifts.note: New segment structure (Cloud & AI + Networking) better reflects Juniper integration; Instant On divested per DOJ

HPE.verdict: Thesis intact: Juniper acquisition is fundamentally sound — Networking is now HPE's growth engine. AI server revenue growing but margin-dilutive. The 2028 $600M synergy target provides a measurable thesis checkpoint.

CRM.headline: FY26 revenue $41.5B +10%; cRPO $35.1B (+16%) outpacing total RPO — near-term demand accelerating; Informatica closed Nov 2025 for agentic data layer.

shifts.trend: Revenue growth: Q3 FY26 +9% → FY26 full year +10%; cRPO +16% suggesting minor acceleration in committed near-term revenue
shifts.note: Agentforce 360 Platform +19% the only double-digit segment; consumption model creates monetization uncertainty

shifts.trend: Operating margin: Q3 FY26 ~20% → FY26 full year 20% (up from 19% FY25); diluted EPS $7.80 vs $6.36
shifts.note: Margin expansion from restructuring and AI investments with AI enabling higher-margin self-service automation

shifts.trend: Informatica acquisition $9.6B (Nov 2025); $12.7B stock buybacks + $1.6B dividends in FY26
shifts.note: Capital allocation split between M&A for AI data layer (Informatica) and aggressive shareholder returns

shifts.trend: Agentforce 360 Platform +19% (Q3 FY26); all other segments +2-10%; cRPO $35.1B (+16%) vs total RPO +14%
shifts.note: AI-driven growth concentrated in Agentforce/Data Cloud; monetization model still 'relatively new and uncertain'

CRM.verdict: Thesis intact but AI monetization upside uncertain. cRPO outpacing total RPO is a positive signal. Agentforce is the right product for AI enterprise workflow but consumption-model revenue recognition remains unpredictable. Operating margin expansion is real.

SNOW.headline: Product revenue +29% consistent across Q3/FY26; RPO $7.9B → $9.8B; NRR stable at 125%; $1M+ customers +27%.

shifts.trend: Product revenue growth: Q3 FY26 +29% YoY → FY26 full year +31% YoY; consistent and not decelerating
shifts.note: Consumption model growth driven by expanding data volumes and AI workload (Cortex, Snowpark ML) adoption

shifts.trend: Forbes Global 2000 customers 790 (43% of revenue); $1M+ customers 733 (Q3) → consistent through FY26
shifts.note: Customer base diversifying with lower customer count concentration than hyperscaler-dependent peers

shifts.trend: Operating cash flow $1.2B FY26; net loss $1.3B GAAP (largely stock-comp); FCF generation improving
shifts.note: Path to GAAP profitability requires stock-comp moderation; adj FCF margin expanding as scale builds

shifts.trend: NRR: Q3 FY26 125% → FY26 125% (stable vs 126% prior year); weighted-avg contract life 2.7 years
shifts.note: NRR stability is key positive — consumption model at scale isn't eroding; $1M+ customer growth +27% is bullish

SNOW.verdict: Thesis intact: Snowflake's AI data layer is real and growing. Consistent 29-31% product revenue growth with stable NRR confirms durable demand. The risk is NRR eventually declining as install base matures — not happening yet.

AMAT.headline: DRAM mix within Semi Systems rising (26% FY25 → 34% Q1 FY26) on HBM transitions; $253M BIS legal settlement hit Q1; Taiwan revenue surging as China normalizes.

shifts.trend: DRAM % of Semi Systems: FY25 26% → Q1 FY26 34% (+8pts); HBM technology-transition spending intensifying
shifts.note: Leading memory (HBM) process-control intensity increase is structural, not cyclical

shifts.trend: China revenue: FY25 $8.5B (-16%) → Q1 FY26 $2.1B (-7%); Taiwan $1.7B (+46%) Q1 FY26 as leading-edge shifts
shifts.note: China normalization ongoing; Taiwan/Korea leading-edge partially offsetting China weakness

shifts.trend: Operating margin: FY25 ~29% → Q1 FY26 26.1% (-4.3pts) from $253M BIS legal settlement charge
shifts.note: One-time settlement creates comps headwind; underlying margin trajectory intact

shifts.trend: $253M BIS export-controls compliance settlement in Q1 FY26 for prior China shipments; CAMT $407M valuation allowance FY25
shifts.note: Regulatory penalties for past China shipments creating ongoing compliance costs

AMAT.verdict: Thesis intact: DRAM/HBM process-control intensity is the multi-year growth driver. BIS settlement is one-time. China normalization manageable as Taiwan/Korea leading-edge picks up. WFE spending on HBM is structurally tied to inference scaling.

GOOGL.headline: Google Cloud $57B FY25 → $20B Q1 2026 (+63%); capex $91B FY25 → $35.7B in Q1 2026 alone (+47% YoY quarter); TPU multi-GW hardware deals beginning.

shifts.trend: Google Cloud: FY25 $57.0B (+31%) → Q1 2026 $20.0B (+63% YoY); Cloud op income $6.6B vs $2.2B in Q1 25
shifts.note: Cloud is now 20%+ of revenue with 33%+ operating margin — becoming a meaningful profit center

shifts.trend: Capex: FY24 $52.5B → FY25 $91.4B; Q1 2026 $35.7B ($143B annualized pace); 2026 full year 'significantly higher'
shifts.note: Biggest capex jump of any hyperscaler in % terms; commitment to AI infrastructure scale is absolute

shifts.trend: TPU hardware multi-GW leasing agreements new in Q1 2026 — first hyperscaler to disclose GW-scale compute leasing
shifts.note: TPU hardware supply agreements to external customers diversifies GOOGL from pure cloud software model

shifts.trend: Cloud operating margin Q1 2025 11% → Q1 2026 33%; Search operating leverage intact
shifts.note: Cloud margin expansion despite heavy capex validates AI workload pricing power at scale

GOOGL.verdict: Thesis intact: Google Cloud acceleration to +63% in Q1 2026 is the biggest positive surprise. TPU hardware deals are a new revenue vector. Capex at $35.7B/quarter is real but Cloud margin expansion confirms the economics work.

MSFT.headline: Azure +39% → +40% YoY; commercial RPO $625B → $627B (doubled YoY); capex $30.9B in Q3 FY26; AI Services highest-margin Azure workload.

shifts.trend: Azure: Q2 FY26 +39% → Q3 FY26 +40% YoY; Microsoft Cloud total: Q2 $54.5B (+29%) → Q3 $54.5B (+29%)
shifts.note: Azure growth sustaining at 40% — no deceleration despite scale; AI Services within Azure growing faster

shifts.trend: Commercial RPO: Q2 FY26 $625B (+110% YoY) → Q3 FY26 $627B (+99% YoY) — contract backlog unprecedented
shifts.note: Largest commercial RPO of any software company; long-dated AI cloud commitments from enterprise and governments

shifts.trend: Q3 FY26 capex $30.9B; FY26 on track for $120B+ full year (vs $56B FY25) — 2x+ YoY increase
shifts.note: Capex primarily Azure GPU/infrastructure; AI Services margin advantage validates economics before utilization peaks

shifts.trend: Intelligent Cloud guided $26.4-26.7B (Q3) → $27.0-27.3B (Q4); growing share of MSFT total revenue
shifts.note: GitHub Copilot, Copilot+ PCs, M365 Copilot all expanding enterprise AI monetization per seat

MSFT.verdict: Thesis strongly intact: Azure at +40% with RPO doubling confirms Microsoft is winning enterprise AI infrastructure. Capex at $120B+ FY26 is manageable given Cloud operating margins. OpenAI partnership remains strategic differentiator.

META.headline: Revenue +22% FY25 → +33% Q1 2026; 2026 capex raised twice: $115-135B → $125-145B; operating margin 43% in Q1 2026.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 +22% YoY → Q1 2026 $56.3B (+33% YoY); ad revenue acceleration on AI Advantage+ automation
shifts.note: AI-powered ad targeting and Reels monetization compounding — pure efficiency play on ad market share

shifts.trend: 2026 capex: FY25 10-K $115-135B → Q1 2026 raised to $125-145B; annualized Q1 capex $79.2B
shifts.note: Two raises in one quarter; Llama, Meta AI, infrastructure build driving capital commitment

shifts.trend: Operating margin: FY25 ~38% → Q1 2026 43%; AI-driven revenue efficiency improving margin faster than capex dilutes
shifts.note: Exceptional: generating 43% operating margin while spending $19.8B/quarter on capex

shifts.trend: Reality Labs: -$4.2B operating loss Q1 2026; Family of Apps: near-100% of operating profit
shifts.note: Reality Labs long-term bet; near-term Meta equity value is 100% advertising + AI Advantage+ story

META.verdict: Thesis strongly intact: META is demonstrating AI returns in real-time — 43% operating margin while raising capex to $125-145B is unprecedented capital efficiency. Revenue acceleration to +33% shows AI ad-targeting improvements are inflecting demand.

AMZN.headline: AWS +17% FY25 → +28% Q1 2026; capex $128B FY25 → $43.2B in Q1 2026 alone; AWS op margin 37.7% record; $100B OpenAI expansion.

shifts.trend: AWS: FY25 ~+17% → Q1 2026 $37.6B (+28% YoY); acceleration driven by AI workload backlog conversion
shifts.note: AWS growth re-accelerating from ~17% to 28% — AI demand converting booked commitments into revenue

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $128.3B; Q1 2026 $43.2B ($172B annualized); 2026 capex expected 'to increase' from $128.3B FY25
shifts.note: Largest absolute capex in world; primarily AWS AI infrastructure (Trainium 3, Inferentia 3)

shifts.trend: AWS operating margin: FY25 ~30% → Q1 2026 37.7%; record margin on AI workload pricing advantage
shifts.note: AWS at 37.7% operating margin while growing 28% — exceptional; ad business and retail improving too

shifts.trend: OpenAI: Q1 2026 announced $100B expansion of existing $38B AWS commitment; AWS custom chips included
shifts.note: OpenAI-AWS relationship deepening: AI model training and inference workloads anchoring AWS AI infrastructure

AMZN.verdict: Thesis strongly intact: AWS re-acceleration to +28% on AI workloads is the key inflection. Record 37.7% AWS margins prove AI workloads are high-value. $100B OpenAI expansion locks in multi-year anchor customer. Capex at $172B+ annualized is the investment for the next growth cycle.

KLAC.headline: Revenue $3.26B Q2 → $3.42B Q3 (+5% sequential); DRAM/HBM process control investment sustained; advanced packaging growing.

shifts.trend: Revenue: Q2 FY26 $3.26B (+27% YoY) → Q3 FY26 $3.42B (+11% YoY); total up on HBM memory investment
shifts.note: YoY% moderation reflects tougher comps; sequential growth continuing; record quarters consecutive

shifts.trend: Memory (DRAM/HBM) investment intensity: Q2 FY26 'memory leading process control growth' → Q3 'HBM leading investments'
shifts.note: HBM stacking complexity requires more KLA inspection steps per wafer — structural intensity increase

shifts.trend: Advanced packaging process control (CoWoS, HBM stacking): described as 'significant growth' in both quarters
shifts.note: Advanced packaging inspection is additive to traditional wafer-level inspection — expands KLA TAM

KLAC.verdict: Thesis intact: KLAC is the direct beneficiary of HBM density scaling — each generation of HBM requires more process control steps. Advanced packaging inspection is a new growth vector. Consecutive record quarters confirm cycle strength.

LRCX.headline: DRAM mix in systems revenue: 34% (Q2) → 39% (Q3); revenue $5.35B → $5.84B (+9% QoQ); HBM etch intensity the core driver.

shifts.trend: DRAM % of systems revenue: Q2 FY26 ~34% → Q3 FY26 39%; foundry weakening, NAND muted
shifts.note: HBM capacity expansion at Samsung/SK Hynix/Micron driving etch tool intensity increase

shifts.trend: Revenue: Q2 FY26 $5.35B (+8% YoY) → Q3 FY26 $5.84B (+9% QoQ, +~36% YoY estimated)
shifts.note: Sequential acceleration driven by DRAM investment surge; YoY growth accelerating sharply

shifts.trend: Foundry soft both quarters (mature node); NAND muted both quarters; DRAM tight both quarters
shifts.note: DRAM/HBM demand absorbing Lam capacity; non-HBM segments providing low-growth floor

LRCX.verdict: Thesis intact: LRCX's etch dominance in DRAM/HBM is the most direct WFE exposure to HBM density scaling. DRAM mix rising from 34% to 39% in one quarter confirms the cycle is accelerating. Foundry and NAND softness is manageable given HBM outperformance.

GEV.headline: RPO $163.3B (+32% YoY); Q1 Gas Power EBITDA +57%; data center prime power orders rising; Prolec GE acquisition adds transformer capacity.

shifts.trend: Gas Power EBITDA: FY25 growing → Q1 2026 +57% YoY; RPO $163.3B (+32% YoY, +9% sequentially)
shifts.note: Hyperscaler prime power orders for gas turbines at GW scale — new demand vector building

shifts.trend: Electrification: Prolec GE acquisition (transformer capacity) + organic switchgear/transformer growth
shifts.note: Data center grid connections require transformers; GEV Electrification segment supply-constrained

shifts.trend: RPO growth: Gas Power equipment/services + Nuclear Power services both building in RPO Q1 2026
shifts.note: Nuclear Power RPO a new growth area as advanced nuclear (BWRX-300 SMR) licensing progresses

GEV.verdict: Thesis intact: GEV is the premier pick for data center power generation via gas turbines. RPO +32% confirms long-duration demand. Gas Power EBITDA +57% validates pricing power. Nuclear optionality (BWRX-300) provides additional upside if SMR permitting accelerates.

VRT.headline: Backlog $7.2B (FY24) → $15.0B (FY25) → still growing in Q1 2026; Americas +53% QoQ; gross margin expanding 400bps.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 $10.2B (+27.7%) → Q1 FY26 $2.65B (+30% YoY); Americas +53% on hyperscaler direct orders
shifts.note: Acceleration in Americas reflects backlog conversion from hyperscaler large-frame orders

shifts.trend: Gross margin: FY25 ~36% → Q1 FY26 +400bps YoY; operating leverage improving on data center mix
shifts.note: Pricing power in supply-constrained liquid cooling and power infrastructure; backlog filled at higher prices

shifts.trend: Backlog: $7.2B (Dec 2024) → $15.0B (Dec 2025) → growing in Q1 2026 (book-to-bill >1.0x)
shifts.note: Demand exceeding supply; hyperscalers placing orders 12-18 months in advance; long-lead components tightening

shifts.trend: Boyd Thermal acquisition (liquid cooling for hyperscale) adds product capability; manufacturing capacity investments ongoing
shifts.note: Liquid cooling mix growing with GPU rack density — from air cooling (~$500/rack) to liquid (~$3000/rack TAM per rack)

VRT.verdict: Thesis strongly intact: VRT is the primary beneficiary of AI data center power and cooling infrastructure. Backlog doubling YoY, Americas +53%, and margin expansion all confirm pricing power and demand durability. Liquid cooling TAM expansion is structural.

NOW.headline: RPO $28.2B (FY25) → $27.7B (Q1 2026, +25% YoY); revenue +21% → +22%; cRPO +25% → +23%; consistent enterprise AI workflow demand.

shifts.trend: Revenue growth: FY25 +21% YoY → Q1 2026 +22% YoY; subscription revenue +22% in Q1 2026
shifts.note: Consistent growth — no acceleration but no deceleration; Now Assist AI agents driving incremental upsell

shifts.trend: cRPO: FY25 +25% → Q1 2026 +23%; RPO: FY25 +27% → Q1 2026 +25%; stable committed revenue picture
shifts.note: Slightly moderating RPO growth but still above revenue growth — forward revenue visibility intact

shifts.trend: US Federal vertical growing; government AI workflow digitization accelerating; 1,000+ Now Assist customers Q1 2026
shifts.note: Government AI adoption is NEW demand; enterprise Pro Plus AI tier expanding monetization per seat

NOW.verdict: Thesis intact: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise AI workflow platform. Consistent +22% revenue growth with cRPO growing faster than revenue confirms durable demand. Now Assist AI agents are driving incremental revenue — government adoption is a new upside vector.

AMD.headline: Data Center: FY25 $16.6B (+32%) → Q1 FY26 $5.8B (+57% YoY); OpenAI 6 GW deal; China export controls ongoing headwind.

shifts.trend: Data Center: FY25 +32% YoY → Q1 FY26 +57% YoY; total revenue FY25 +34% → Q1 FY26 +38%
shifts.note: MI350 GPU ramp + OpenAI 6 GW commitment accelerating. DC now 56% of total revenue Q1 FY26

shifts.trend: China: FY25 22% of revenue ($7.8B); $800M MI308 charge Q2 FY25, partially reversed Q4; Feb 2026 MI325 license with 25% tariff
shifts.note: China export control overhang persisting into FY26; tariff adds complexity to any China shipments

shifts.trend: Gross margin: FY25 50% → Q1 FY26 53% (+3pts YoY); Data Center mix expansion primary driver
shifts.note: Margin expansion despite $440M net China export charges in FY25; clean underlying margin improving

shifts.trend: R&D +39% to $2.4B in Q1 FY26; Helios rack-scale platform investment; ZT Systems integration complete
shifts.note: Building AI rack capability internally vs NVDA's NVLink approach; OpenAI 6 GW demand validates strategy

AMD.verdict: Thesis intact: AMD Data Center +57% in Q1 FY26 confirms hyperscaler adoption of MI350. OpenAI 6 GW deal creates structural demand. China overhang limits upside vs NVDA but FY26 DC trajectory looks strong without China contribution.

COHR.headline: D&C segment +41% in both FQ2 and FQ3 FY26; segment profit +49% in FQ3; 800G volumes mature, 1.6T qualification in progress.

shifts.trend: D&C revenue: FQ2 FY26 +41% → FQ3 FY26 +41% YoY; total revenue FQ2 +21% → FQ3 +21% — consistent
shifts.note: AI datacenter demand sustaining at 41% growth for two consecutive quarters — no sign of deceleration

shifts.trend: Segment profit: FQ3 FY26 $348M (+49% YoY) vs revenue +41% — operating leverage building
shifts.note: D&C segment margin expanding as 800G volumes scale; 1.6T transition will create next ASP uplift

shifts.trend: Supply chain normalization post-COVID; wafer and substrate supply no longer constraining in either quarter
shifts.note: 1.6T (200G-lane) transceiver qualification in progress — next product cycle under development

COHR.verdict: Thesis intact: COHR's 41% D&C segment growth for two consecutive quarters confirms durable AI datacenter optical demand. Operating leverage improving. 1.6T ramp in CY2026 is the next growth catalyst. Scale-across interconnect for AI clusters is the incremental TAM.

LITE.headline: Revenue +90% YoY in FQ3 FY26; cloud transceivers +$137M; OCS $25M+ initial phase; supply relief coming online.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FQ2 FY26 estimated +30% YoY → FQ3 FY26 $808.4M (+90.1% YoY) — acceleration driven by cloud demand
shifts.note: FQ3 jump reflects both volume growth and new OCS product category starting to contribute

shifts.trend: FQ2 supply allocation language: 'demand outpacing supply' → FQ3: supply investment coming online, constraints easing
shifts.note: Manufacturing capacity additions easing the supply-demand imbalance that was limiting revenue

shifts.trend: OCS (optical circuit switch): FQ2 initial phase → FQ3 $25M+ — new product category ramping
shifts.note: OCS is a structurally new product with AI fabric switching potential; margin profile likely above transceivers

LITE.verdict: Thesis intact and accelerating: LITE's +90% YoY in FQ3 2026 confirms it's in the sweet spot of the 800G AI datacenter build. OCS is a new product category that expands TAM. Supply relief coming online should sustain growth through FQ4 2026 and beyond.

ANET.headline: Revenue +29% FY25 → +35% Q1 2026; product deferred revenue jumping on AI cluster acceptance periods; 1.6T (Atlas) in hyperscaler qualification.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 +28.6% → Q1 2026 +35.1% YoY; product deferred revenue increasing on acceptance timing
shifts.note: AI cluster acceptance periods create revenue timing mismatch — demand is ahead of recognized revenue

shifts.trend: Two customers: FY25 42% combined → Q1 2026 still >40% combined; hyperscaler dependence persistent
shifts.note: Concentration risk is real but ANET has won AI networking from both Ethernet-first hyperscalers

shifts.trend: 1.6T switching (Atlas) in hyperscaler qualification Q1 2026 — next-gen product transition underway
shifts.note: 1.6T Atlas switches will drive next ASP uplift and extend AI networking addressable market beyond 800G

ANET.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening: ANET +35% in Q1 2026 with 1.6T in hyperscaler qualification confirms it has won the AI Ethernet switch market. Deferred revenue growth reflects genuine demand. 1.6T transition in 2026 is the next growth catalyst.

PLTR.headline: US Commercial +67% FY25 → +95% Q1 2026; total revenue +56% → +85%; GAAP profitable; AIP bootcamp flywheel working.

shifts.trend: Total revenue: FY25 +56% → Q1 2026 +85% YoY; US Commercial: FY25 +67% → Q1 2026 +95% YoY
shifts.note: Acceleration in both total and US Commercial segments — AIP adoption is compounding, not plateauing

shifts.trend: GAAP profitable FY25 (first full-year GAAP profit); GAAP profitable Q1 2026; adj operating margin >25%
shifts.note: GAAP profitability sustained through high-growth quarter — operational leverage demonstrated

shifts.trend: Government: FY25 +~30% → Q1 2026 +76% YoY; Commercial: FY25 +67% → Q1 2026 +95% YoY — both accelerating
shifts.note: Government AI acceleration (TITAN, Maven) adds to Commercial AIP momentum — broad-based acceleration

PLTR.verdict: Thesis strongly intact: PLTR's 85% revenue growth in Q1 2026 with GAAP profitability is exceptional. US Commercial +95% confirms AIP bootcamp → production deployment flywheel is working at scale. Government +76% adds durable long-cycle revenue. Most compelling software name in the dashboard.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions +24% → +51% YoY in consecutive quarters; Reverse Morris Trust announced to create pure-play data center thermal company.

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions: Q2 FY26 +24% → Q3 FY26 +51% YoY; data center product sales +$130M QoQ in Q3
shifts.note: Data center thermal management demand compounding; NA and Europe hyperscale cited as drivers

shifts.trend: Reverse Morris Trust: Performance Technologies spun/merged with Gentherm; MOD retains Climate Solutions as pure-play
shifts.note: Strategic pivot validates data center thesis — management concentrating capital on highest-growth segment

MOD.verdict: Thesis intact: MOD's pure-play transformation via the Gentherm merger is the right strategic move. Climate Solutions +51% confirms AI data center liquid cooling demand acceleration. Management signaling conviction in the data center vertical.

SNPS.headline: FY25 revenue $7.05B (+15% incl $757M Ansys); Q1 FY26 $2.41B (+66% reported, +8% organic); Design IP weak; $3.5B term loan paid off.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 +15% (including partial-year Ansys) → Q1 FY26 +66% reported (+8% organic ex-Ansys); Ansys distorts YoY
shifts.note: Organic EDA growth ~8% — solid but not accelerating; Ansys simulation adds a new product family

shifts.trend: $3.5B term loan paid off Q1 FY26; $13.5B total debt at FY25-end being aggressively reduced
shifts.note: Debt reduction priority: buyback suspended until leverage normalized; post-merger balance sheet repair

shifts.trend: Design IP: FY25 China restrictions + foundry softness + roadmap missteps → -6% Q1 FY26 YoY
shifts.note: Design IP negative growth for 2+ consecutive quarters — structural or temporary depends on China recovery

SNPS.verdict: Thesis intact but complex: organic EDA growth 8% is solid. Ansys acquisition is strategically correct for AI chip simulation. Design IP weakness is the concern — if structural, margin compression continues. Debt paydown is positive.

INTC.headline: DCAI +17% FY25 → +22% Q1 2026; Intel Foundry losses persisting ($8.1B FY25, $2.4B Q1); supply constraints limiting Q1; 18A ramp in 2H 2026.

shifts.trend: DCAI: FY25 +17% YoY → Q1 2026 $9.2B (+22% YoY); total revenue FY25 -1% → Q1 2026 +7%
shifts.note: DCAI acceleration driven by Gaudi 3 GPU ramp and EPYC server CPU market share gains

shifts.trend: Q1 2026: market demand exceeded available supply — first time INTC cited supply constraints in recent years
shifts.note: Supply constraints limiting revenue in Q1 2026; expected to persist 1H 2026

shifts.trend: Intel Foundry: FY25 $8.1B operating loss → Q1 2026 $2.4B loss (not improving); 14A uncertain if no external customer
shifts.note: Foundry is the existential question: without an external win on 14A, Intel may abandon leading-edge process

INTC.verdict: Thesis impaired: DCAI +22% is positive but Intel Foundry losses ($2.4B/quarter) are the structural problem. Without a 14A external customer, Intel's manufacturing strategy may pivot entirely to TSMC — which removes the IDM 2.0 thesis differentiation. 18A Panther Lake launch in 2H 2026 is the critical milestone.

NVT.headline: Revenue +29.5% FY25 → +53.5% Q1 FY26; Systems Protection +46% organic; backlog growing in both quarters.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 +29.5% → Q1 FY26 +53.5% reported, +34.4% organic; Systems Protection: FY25 strong → Q1 +76% reported
shifts.note: Data center enclosure and liquid cooling demand accelerating — organic growth alone at 34%+ is exceptional

shifts.trend: Backlog and order book expanding in both quarters; select pricing power on supply-constrained product lines
shifts.note: Hyperscalers placing 12+ month advance orders; supply tight in liquid cooling and custom enclosure products

NVT.verdict: Thesis intact: NVT's +34% organic growth in Q1 FY26 confirms durable data center demand for its enclosure/thermal products. Liquid cooling TAM expanding with GPU rack density. Backlog growth sustains revenue visibility through 2026.

ETN.headline: Electrical Americas backlog $14.5B (+44% YoY); orders +42% organic; book-to-bill 1.2x; Boyd Thermal liquid cooling added.

shifts.trend: Backlog: prior year → Q1 2026 $14.5B (+44% YoY); book-to-bill 1.2x; data center order velocity high
shifts.note: Electrical switchgear, circuit breakers, transformers for data center power distribution — multi-quarter backlog

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas organic: strong in FY25 → Q1 2026 orders +42% organic; sales growing 10-15%
shifts.note: Data center commercial/institutional market strength offsetting utility and industrial weakness

ETN.verdict: Thesis intact: ETN's $14.5B Electrical Americas backlog (+44% YoY) provides multi-year revenue visibility. Data center power distribution is a multi-quarter fulfillment business. Boyd Thermal addition extends into liquid cooling. Infrastructure cycle duration is 5-10 years.

CMI.headline: Power Systems +19% Q1 FY26 to $1.96B with 29.5% EBITDA margin (from 23.6%); data center prime power demand 6-8 quarters out.

shifts.trend: Power Systems: FY25 strong growth → Q1 FY26 +19% YoY with EBITDA margin 29.5% (from 23.6%)
shifts.note: Data center power generation demand driving EBITDA margin expansion — premium product mix

shifts.trend: Data center demand extends 6-8 quarters forward in both FY25 and Q1 FY26 — backlog fully booked
shifts.note: Generator set demand from data center customers locked into Cummins order books through mid-2027

CMI.verdict: Thesis intact: Cummins Power Systems is riding the AI data center prime power cycle with 29.5% EBITDA margin and a 6-8 quarter backlog. The thesis is: AI clusters need generator backup power, and Cummins has dominant position in high-power gensets.

LEU.headline: Backlog grew from $2.3B (FY25) to $3.9B (Q1 2026); Centrus-Oklo HALEU JV announced; Fluor EPC contract for HALEU facility.

shifts.trend: Backlog: FY25 $2.3B contingent → Q1 2026 $3.9B total ($3.1B LEU incl $2.4B contingent) — +70% growth
shifts.note: HALEU enrichment milestone secured; utility contracts for standard LEU expanding on Russian supply ban

shifts.trend: HALEU: FY25 demonstration → Q1 2026 Fluor EPC contract + Centrus-Oklo JV announced
shifts.note: HALEU commercialization pathway accelerating in Q1 2026 with real construction and partnership milestones

LEU.verdict: Thesis intact: LEU's backlog nearly doubled in one quarter — the Russian LEU supply ban is driving US utility demand for domestic supply. HALEU milestones (Fluor EPC + Oklo JV) de-risk the advanced fuel cycle thesis. Low revenue base but high option value on HALEU commercialization.

CAT.headline: Q1 2026 revenues $17.4B (+22%); Power & Energy +22% on data center prime power; backlog building from AI data center orders.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 +4.3% → Q1 2026 +22% YoY; Power & Energy $7.0B (+22%) driven by data center prime power
shifts.note: Prime power demand from data center customers is accelerating CAT Power & Energy beyond prior cycle expectations

shifts.trend: Prime power order trends: FY25 'starting to see orders trend higher' → Q1 2026 'robust backlog growth'
shifts.note: Hyperscalers ordering CAT generators for alternative/on-site power solutions at GW scale

CAT.verdict: Thesis intact: CAT's Power & Energy +22% in Q1 2026 with robust backlog validates the AI prime power thesis. Data center demand is becoming a structural tailwind for Caterpillar — not cyclical. $2.2-2.4B tariff headwind is manageable against the demand backdrop.

HUBB.headline: Electrical Solutions organic +10.6% Q1 2026 consistently driven by data center; FY26 guidance raised.

shifts.trend: Electrical Solutions organic: FY25 data center/light industrial growth → Q1 2026 +10.6% organic consistently
shifts.note: Steady data center electrical component demand; wiring devices and circuit protection for power distribution

shifts.trend: FY26 guidance raised after Q1 beat in both revenue and margin
shifts.note: Conservative management raising guidance reflects visible data center demand in backlog

HUBB.verdict: Thesis intact: HUBB is a steady data center electrical infrastructure beneficiary. Organic +10.6% in Q1 2026 is consistent and durable. Not a high-growth play but provides stable exposure to data center power distribution. FY26 guidance raise is positive.

PWR.headline: Backlog: $28.5B revenue FY25 → Q1 $48.47B backlog and $26.24B RPO (+10% QoQ); data center EPC the primary driver.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 $28.5B (+20.3%) → Q1 2026 $7.87B (+26% YoY); backlog +10% in one quarter
shifts.note: Data center campus construction (EPC, electrical, site prep) is adding to Quanta's utility contracting revenue

shifts.trend: Backlog: FY25 large → Q1 2026 $48.47B; RPO +10.4% sequentially to $26.24B
shifts.note: Electrician labor constraint is the primary capacity limit; Quanta workforce training programs expanding supply

PWR.verdict: Thesis intact: PWR's $48.47B backlog at +10% quarterly growth rate provides exceptional revenue visibility. Data center EPC is the fastest-growing end-market. Labor supply is the limiting factor — Quanta's training programs are the moat.

GNRC.headline: C&I +28% Q1 2026 on data center demand; large-MW products gaining acceptance; segments reorganized to show C&I separately.

shifts.trend: C&I: FY25 'supply constrained' → Q1 2026 +28% to $510M; reorganized to dedicated C&I segment for visibility
shifts.note: Large-MW C&I generator products for data center prime/backup power gaining market acceptance

shifts.trend: FY25 supply-constrained → Q1 2026 supply easing as Generac expands C&I production capacity
shifts.note: Supply constraints that limited FY25 revenue are beginning to ease in Q1 2026

GNRC.verdict: Thesis intact: GNRC's C&I segment +28% driven by data center customers confirms the backup power thesis. Large-MW products entering volume production is the next growth step. Residential segment volatile (weather-driven) but C&I data center is the durable thread.

AMKR.headline: All four end markets growing Q1 2026; computing +19% on datacenter packaging; capex $225M (vs $80M YoY); advanced packaging capacity investment.

shifts.trend: Q1 2026: All four end markets positive; communications +42%, automotive +28%, computing +19%, consumer +4%
shifts.note: Broadest multi-market growth in recent history; datacenter AI packaging driving computing segment

shifts.trend: Capex: FY25 ~$80M/quarter → Q1 2026 $225M (+181%) for advanced packaging capacity
shifts.note: Advanced packaging (2.5D, HBM integration, chiplet-in-package) requires significant capital investment

AMKR.verdict: Thesis intact: AMKR's all-market growth in Q1 2026 is exceptional. Computing +19% from datacenter AI packaging and capex tripling for advanced packaging both confirm the AI chip packaging cycle is real and multi-year. Apple/Qualcomm concentration is stable.

CLS.headline: HPS +81% FY25 → +63% Q1 FY26; HPS now 42% of revenue; hyperscaler AI switch programs primary driver.

shifts.trend: HPS: FY25 +81% (to $5.0B) → Q1 FY26 +63% (to ~$1.7B); total revenue: FY25 strong → Q1 +53%
shifts.note: HPS networking switch programs with hyperscalers are the primary driver of CLS growth

shifts.trend: HPS mix: FY24 29% → FY25 41% → Q1 FY26 42% of total revenue — structural mix shift continuing
shifts.note: HPS becoming dominant revenue segment; CCS (compute/communications) also growing at 76% Q1 2026

CLS.verdict: Thesis intact: CLS is the contract manufacturer for AI networking switch programs at hyperscalers. HPS +63% with 42% revenue concentration confirms the AI switch buildout is sustaining. Revenue visibility is multi-quarter given hyperscaler capex commitments.

CDNS.headline: Revenue +14% FY25 → +19% Q1 2026; China +36% Q1 2026; backlog $8.0B; BIS restrictions rescinded Jul 2025.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 +14.1% → Q1 2026 +19% YoY; China $189M (+36% YoY) in Q1 2026 post-BIS rescission
shifts.note: Growth acceleration in Q1 2026 driven by AI chip design demand and recovering China revenues

shifts.trend: BIS 'is-informed' letter (May 2025) → rescinded July 2, 2025; China revenue recovering strongly in Q1 2026
shifts.note: BIS rescission removed key overhang; China EDA revenues recovering faster than expected

CDNS.verdict: Thesis intact: CDNS +19% in Q1 2026 with China +36% confirms the BIS overhang is fully resolved. AI chip design complexity is structural demand. Backlog $8.0B with $0.7B non-cancelable provides revenue visibility. Palladium emulation (fastest-growing product) validates AI-chip verification demand.

CRWV.headline: Revenue $1.9B → Q1 2026 $2.08B (+112% YoY); Microsoft concentration 67% → 45%; RPO $98.8B; Meta and OpenAI scaling.

shifts.trend: Microsoft % of revenue: FY25 67% → Q1 2026 ~45%; Meta and OpenAI ramp driving diversification
shifts.note: Concentration risk declining rapidly as new customers (Meta, OpenAI) ramp on CoreWeave GPU clusters

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 +168% → Q1 2026 $2.08B (+112% YoY); RPO $98.8B stable (full long-term backlog intact)
shifts.note: Growth rate moderating from 168% to 112% but still exceptional; base effect from large FY25 numbers

CRWV.verdict: Thesis intact but concentration was the primary risk — now easing. CoreWeave's GPU-cloud model with $98.8B RPO confirms multi-year revenue visibility. Microsoft declining from 67% to 45% in one quarter is a major de-risking. OpenAI $6.5B and Meta relationships now material.

FN.headline: DCI revenue +90.4% FQ3; telecom +42.5%; laser chip ramp broad-based; total revenue +39% YoY.

shifts.trend: Total revenue: FQ2 +29% → FQ3 +39.3% YoY; DCI (hyperscaler 1.6T-cycle): FQ2 growing → FQ3 +90.4% YoY
shifts.note: DCI acceleration driven by 1.6T hyperscaler qualifications converting to volume — step-change quarter

shifts.trend: Components (laser chips): FQ3 accelerating on broad-based demand across intra-DC, DCI, long-haul
shifts.note: Laser chip ramp for 800G transceivers is additive to telecom/DCI module revenue — TAM expanding

FN.verdict: Thesis intact: FN's DCI +90% and total revenue +39% in FQ3 2026 confirms the 1.6T AI datacenter optical cycle is converting from qualification to volume. Laser chip component business is growing alongside module revenue, expanding FN's TAM and vertical integration.

MXL.headline: Revenue +43% Q1 2026 vs trough; infrastructure optical +$36.3M; 800G PAM4 DSP shipments; 1.6T design wins in qualification.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 recovering → Q1 2026 $137.2M (+43% YoY); infrastructure optical leading growth
shifts.note: Recovery from FY24 inventory trough accelerating; 800G PAM4 DSP volumes ramping

shifts.trend: Infrastructure net revenue +$36.3M: high-performance analog optical, wireless backhaul/access growing
shifts.note: 800G→1.6T interconnect transition creates demand inflection for MXL's DSP silicon

MXL.verdict: Thesis intact: MXL is recovering from inventory trough with +43% growth driven by 800G optical volumes. 1.6T design wins in qualification position MXL for the next cycle. Residential weakness offset by infrastructure optical strength — the correct mix shift for AI networking exposure.

OKLO.headline: Meta prepayment agreement for 1.2 GW (Jan 2026); Centrus-Oklo HALEU JV (Mar 2026); Fluor EPC; first commercial deployment target 2028 unchanged.

shifts.trend: Investing cash: FY25 9M $605.9M → FY26 guidance $350-450M total; Meta prepayment provides incremental funding
shifts.note: Meta prepayment agreement provides cash for 1.2 GW Pike County campus development — first revenue pathway

shifts.trend: Key milestones FY25→Q1 2026: Meta 1.2 GW prepayment (Jan) + Centrus HALEU JV (Mar) + NRC Phase I no gaps
shifts.note: Three major milestones in 6 months — commercial development acceleration is real

OKLO.verdict: Thesis intact: Oklo's 2028 target is holding while the commercial pathway is building with real hyperscaler agreements (Meta 1.2 GW). HALEU JV with Centrus addresses fuel supply. Order book 18,100 MWe. Primary risk: NRC licensing timeline and first Aurora deployment execution.

CEG.headline: Calpine closed Jan 7, 2026 at ~$22B; combined 55 GW; Crane (TMI) DOE $1B loan guarantee; Q1 2026 first Calpine-consolidated quarter.

shifts.trend: FY25 rev $25.5B standalone → Q1 2026 substantially higher as Calpine ($27 GW) consolidates; Adj EPS $9.39 FY25
shifts.note: Calpine adds gas dispatch revenue and retail load; combined company revenue run-rate $35B+

shifts.trend: Nuclear PTC revenue: FY24 high → FY25 lower on power price mix → FY26 Calpine consolidation changes picture
shifts.note: New AI/data center load contracts (Meta/Microsoft/Google) locking in premium nuclear baseload pricing

shifts.trend: Calpine acquisition: $4.5B cash + 50M shares (~$22B total); leverage ratio increased materially post-close
shifts.note: Integration of $22B acquisition creates near-term leverage; Calpine FCF helps debt service

CEG.verdict: Thesis intact: CEG's Calpine acquisition transforms it into the largest private power producer at 55 GW with both nuclear baseload (stable, ESG) and natural gas dispatch (flexible, high-margin). AI/data center nuclear PPAs (Meta, Microsoft) validate the long-duration power thesis.
